title: Readiness Audit, Prior-Skill Retrieval, and Evidence Baseline · Mastery Review
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for readiness audit, prior-skill retrieval, and evidence baseline and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for mastery baseline and project readiness ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.
Distinguish reusable evidence, practice residue, generated output, sensitive material, and unsupported claims in an existing work sample.
Reproduce one prior behavior under a changed input, environment, or constraint and retain its exact pass or failure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Competency Inventory
steps.instruction:
Order the Competency Inventory evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Competency Inventory.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Competency Inventory assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Competency Inventory, keep Competency Inventory connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A learner claims independent mastery from guided exercises, but a changed environment breaks the artifact and the evidence ledger has no dated reproduction. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Competency Inventory has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Competency Inventory, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Competency Inventory precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Competency Inventory, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A learner claims independent mastery from guided exercises, but a changed environment breaks the artifact and the evidence ledger has no dated reproduction. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Competency Inventory, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Course completion badges prove every underlying skill remains retrievable.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ledger
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Evidence Ledger: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A learner claims independent mastery from guided exercises, but a changed environment breaks the artifact and the evidence ledger has no dated reproduction. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Create a revision-bound ledger linking each claim to artifact, verification, changed case, limitation, and owner.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ledger.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ledger, keep Evidence Ledger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a revision-bound ledger linking each claim to artifact, verification, changed case, limitation, and owner.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
